description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.

Req Targeting Scope Cyberware, otherwise won't fire. If miss single attack by 4 or less, roll second attack at same DV 1d10+10 (not normal stat). Can add luck. Target can dodge the second roll as normal if able to do so.
Turns weapon into a "Smartgun". Refer to all rules specified on pg. 344 before using.
Access to nervous system.
Plating on your hand which allows you to utilise Smartguns. Requires a neural link.
An artificial eye. Choose Kiroshi Optics!
Provides a +1 to aimed shots.
A very heavy pistol. It can't be concealed. +1 to Attack.
Single socket for installation and removal of a piece of chipware.
Allows you to see detail up to 800m/yds. +1 to Single Shot/Aimed Shots for targets greater than 50m/yds.
Turns weapon into a "Smartgun". Refer to all rules specified on pg. 344 before using.
Must be paired. Provides immunity to dazzling effects (like flashbangs).
Increase Body by 2 (and related stats). Stacks. Max at 10 Body.
Armor Item for Subdermal Armor Cyberware.
A long-distance sniper rifle. Holds 4 bullets in a magazine. +1 to Attack.

A direct upgrade/replacement for skill chips. When installing, Humanity is lost by skill category instead of by skill. When installed into a Chipware Socket, a Skill Chip adds +3 to the skill it was made for. Additionally, that skill receives a 20% I.P. discount when further trained. Multiple skill chips for the same skill do not stack.
A sight midification for any ranged weapon. Provides +1 to hit against targets within 12m.

Req Targeting Scope Cyberware, otherwise won't fire. If miss single attack by 4 or less, roll second attack at same DV 1d10+10 (not normal stat). Can add luck. Target can dodge the second roll as normal if able to do so.

Generic Excellent Qulaity Light Melee weapon.

+1 to Attack.
+2 Grooming with Chemskin. Color changing hair.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 4X/Isolated Elite Threat - Iron Sights members are specialized agents, who tend to work alone or in pairs.

Gang-Wide Ability - Concealed Identity: When placing one or more Iron Sights agents in a scene, they may disguise themselves flawlessly as any other type of mook, up to and including the acquisition of additional gear which the mook would normally be carrying. If they do so, their name changes to the other mook, they gain that mook's abilities in addition to their own, and are indistinguishable from a normal enemy with extra gear and abilities unless another character succeeds a DV21 Deduction or Conceal/Reveal Object check while specifically looking at/for cybernetics, or a DV24 Cybertech check while interacting with them.

Ability - Assassin's Toolbox: When entering a scene, it should be assumed that each hitman has full control over all electronics and machines in that scene which are not owned by a player character, accessed either by their agent or by the control panel for the device.

Gear Note: Shoulder Arms chipware is already considered when calculating martial skill. Double check to make sure relevant bonuses from smartgun link, sights, and targeting scope are applied.

Every few weeks a news story will break about an attempted assassination. Sometimes they get away with it, sometimes the subdermal stops it. Always a smart round fired from a certain kind of sniper rifle. No one's been able to catch who's been doing it. They just disappear like a ghost…
